title: aita for 'ruining our family trip'
text: my older sister is a primary school teacher and so, she's on vacation at the moment (just for reference i live in south africa, and we run on our own school calendars over here) for the whole of august, meanwhile i'm in my 'on season' at university/collage meaning that i have (online) classes to attend and assignments to get through.  my family inherited some time share up in the pilanesberg game reserve that comes up more or less three times a year or so at random, and it just so happens that this week is one of the weeks that it’s come up as available for the family.  since my older sister is on holiday at the moment, she’s going to take the time share and go down and she’s persuaded our mom to go with her, so it was going to just me and my dad for the next three days.  this morning my dad called me and said that he could probably swing it so he can take the 3 days off of work since he owns his own business. (the man works himself to the bone year in and year out so it's a small rarity for him to take time off) and the prospect of my dad going got my sister all exited at the prospect of it being a family trip. my problem is, i have work to do! i'm in the middle of a semester and have tests to write and lectures to attend and in the middle of a game reserve, connection is pretty hard to come by so i would essentially be missing out on three days of work, and my uni isn't about to stop for the next 3 days so just so i can take a vacation. i told my dad as much and, while he was kinda bummed out, he understood.  word has now gotten to my sister that i'm not willing to go and he is heckling me constantly, telling me i can 'just take the three days off, you'll only miss a few days of work, and you can stay up late to catch up after we're back' even if i were to go and i did work while i was there, it would be a huge distraction for me, and there’s no signal so i would be missing large chunks of my syllabus. i know myself, if i go, i will fall behind, but my sister keeps telling me that i'm 'selfish' and 'only saying no because i don't want her to be happy' because she wants us to go away together (which we haven't done in quite a few years)  she's right, technically i could go, but it would be at the expense of my grades and my work schedule for at least the next two weeks as i would have to find time to catch everything up. i feel guilty but i'm sticking to my gut and refusing to go away and bunk uni. so... am i the asshole for being selfish, 'ruining a family trip'?

storyboard.content: My older sister is a primary school teacher on vacation for the whole of August, while I'm in the middle of my university semester with online classes to attend and assignments to complete.
My family inherited a timeshare at a game reserve that comes up about three times a year at random. This week it became available, and my sister persuaded our mom to go.
My dad, who works himself to the bone and rarely takes time off, said he could take 3 days off to go. This got my sister excited about making it a family trip.
I told my dad I couldn't go because I'm in the middle of a semester with tests to write and lectures to attend. He was bummed but understood.
In the middle of a game reserve, internet connection is hard to come by, so I would essentially be missing out on three days of work. My university isn't going to stop for me.
My sister has been heckling me constantly, telling me I can 'just take the three days off' and stay up late to catch up after we're back.
My sister keeps telling me that I'm 'selfish' and 'only saying no because I don't want her to be happy' because she wants us to go away together as a family.
I know myself -- if I go, I will fall behind. It would be at the expense of my grades and my work schedule for at least the next two weeks.
